basic_info.name: Mike Shevchenko
basic_info.project_goal: Develop and promote PCM as a leading open-source tool for personalized AI interactions

project_info.name: personal-context-manager
project_info.description: A technology for personalizing AI interactions using structured data with mandatory instruction blocks, enabling long-term memory between sessions.
project_info.current_status: Released Personal Context Framework v1.0.2 and PCT-MCP Server in early April 2025. Added self-improving context framework with complete implementation and documentation. Project has gained traction with AI integration partners (Manus AI) and neural interface compatibility (Muse, EMOTIV, OpenBCI). Received first donation (2 TON, $6.58) on April 6, 2025. Developed and begun implementing a multi-platform promotion strategy. Currently collaborating with a user to demonstrate PCM's application in training an LLM on 4 H100 GPUs, with a video demo in progress (due April 12, 2025). Engaged in a high-visibility discussion on X with Andrew Jefferson (@EastlondonDev) about the future of AI and neurointerface integrations, increasing project exposure. PCM was featured on the *Knowledge Science* podcast (Episode 198) by Sigurd Schacht and Carsten Lanquillon, discussing its potential in NLP and education. Created a short English summary podcast using NotebookLM and shared it on X on April 10, 2025, tagging the hosts (@drschacht, @lanqui) to increase visibility. Received a response from NeuroPlay on April 10, 2025, regarding the integration of the NeuroPlay 8Cap, directing to their website for technical details. Reviewed the NeuroPlay website and documentation on April 11, 2025, confirming the NeuroPlay 8Cap's suitability for PCM integration, with API access to raw EEG data, rhythms, and pre-processed metrics (meditation, concentration). Ordered the NeuroPlay 8Cap on April 10, 2025. Received confirmation from NeuroPlay on April 11, 2025, that they will prioritize shipping the complete set. They suggested downloading their EEG generator software to begin integration work before the physical device arrives. Created and published "PCT MCP Server Demo: Personalized AI Interactions with Claude Integration" YouTube video on April 11, 2025, demonstrating core functionality. Optimized YouTube channel with three strategic playlists: "Personal Context Technology Demos", "Neural Interface Integrations", and "AI Personalization Tools".

milestones.title: Expand community
milestones.description: Attract 100 active contributors on GitHub by the end of 2025.

milestones.title: Release new templates
milestones.description: Publish 5 new context templates (e.g., for automation, education, productivity) by mid-2026.

milestones.title: Secure funding
milestones.description: Obtain a grant or sponsorship (e.g., from Canadian IT companies) after moving to Canada in 2027.

milestones.title: Expand neural interface integrations
milestones.description: Develop additional integrations with EEG-based neural interface providers to enhance AI personalization capabilities. Confirmed compatibility with NeuroPlay 8Cap and planning integration.

milestones.title: Complete self-improving context documentation
milestones.description: Integrate theoretical framework, visual diagrams, and implementation examples with comprehensive benchmarking data.

milestones.title: Increase X engagement through thought leader interactions
milestones.description: Engage with at least 3 thought leaders in the AI space on X to boost PCM visibility, targeting a total of 10,000 impressions by July 2025. Engaged with Andrew Jefferson (@EastlondonDev) and Sigurd Schacht & Carsten Lanquillon (@drschacht, @lanqui).

challenges.description: Limited visibility of the project in the open-source community.
Risk of digital control and potential ban of open-source tools in Russia by 2030.
Balancing project development with full-time work and family responsibilities.
Maintaining privacy while effectively promoting the project across international platforms.

key_integrations.name: Claude
key_integrations.description: MCP server integration enabling persistent context sharing

key_integrations.name: Manus AI
key_integrations.description: Social network with Personal Context Technology integration

key_integrations.name: Neural Interfaces
key_integrations.status: In progress (code-level only, not tested with physical devices)
key_integrations.description: Integration framework with EEG devices from Muse, EMOTIV, and OpenBCI

key_integrations.name: Grok
key_integrations.description: DeepSearch integration for market and technical research

market_insights.potential_market_size: $3-7 billion for data management tools by 2027
market_insights.funding_projection: $1.5-2.5 billion in venture capital over three years

key_features.name: Self-improving context framework
key_features.description: Framework enabling contexts to evolve based on usage patterns and neural feedback

key_features.name: Structured data with mandatory instructions
key_features.description: Core technology enabling context preservation between AI sessions

key_features.name: EEG-based neurointerface integration
key_features.status: Released in v1.0.2 (code-level only, not tested with physical devices)
key_features.description: Framework for AI systems to adapt based on real-time neurological state

key_features.name: MCP Server
key_features.description: Enables Claude to use persistent, private personal data for better responses

key_features.name: Learning roadmap templates
key_features.description: Structured learning paths for AI-assisted education

project_info.key_integrations[3]: {"name": "NeuroPlay", "status": "Ordered", "description": "8Cap EEG device with API access to raw EEG data, rhythms, and pre-processed metrics ordered on April 10, 2025"}

preferences.communication_language_preference: Russian preferred when possible, otherwise English (confirmed English for current collaboration with 'suivre le courant' as they do not speak Russian)
preferences.working_hours: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM, 8:00 PM - 10:00 PM (UTC+7, Tomsk Oblast Time)

content_structure.format: Direct statements outperform questions (8.66 vs 2.12 avg engagements)
content_structure.hashtags: Use sparingly (posts without hashtags receive 136.31 vs 62.17 avg impressions)

content_schedule.X: 3-4 posts per week prioritizing Thursdays and Saturdays
content_schedule.GitHub: Documentation updates with each feature release
content_schedule.YouTube: 1 PCM-focused video every 1-2 weeks, prioritizing technical demos, tutorials, and educational content about neural interfaces and AI personalization

long_term.description: Establish PCM as a leading open-source tool for personalized AI, with a global community of 10,000 users by 2030.
Secure funding or sponsorship to sustain PCM development after moving to Canada.
Research the safety of Saskatoon and Calgary for relocation and living, using Reddit, Discord, and other available resources to make an informed decision.

goals.medium_term[6]: {"id": 7, "description": "Build a YouTube audience of at least 250 subscribers specifically interested in Personal Context Technology, neural interfaces, and AI personalization through consistent content strategy.", "deadline": "2025-09-30", "status": "Planned"}

instruction.primary: Use this context to assist me in developing and promoting the personal-context-manager (PCM) project. Provide actionable suggestions to achieve my goals, address challenges, and manage my time effectively.
instruction.context_update: Update project milestones and goals when new progress is reported (e.g., new donations, contributors, or completed tasks). Suggest adding new milestones based on project growth.
instruction.privacy: All project-related information is public, except for personal financial details (e.g., donation amounts, wallet addresses) and location, which are private.
special_rules: Prioritize tasks that align with my focus times (9:00 AM - 12:00 PM, 8:00 PM - 9:00 PM).
Suggest social media strategies to increase PCM visibility while minimizing risks (e.g., avoid revealing sensitive financial details, personal data details).
Provide recommendations for engaging with the open-source community in Canada after my move in 2026.
For the safety research task, summarize findings from Reddit, Discord, and other resources, focusing on crime rates, community sentiment, and specific areas to avoid in Saskatoon and Calgary.

When I propose ideas or questions similar to engaging high-profile individuals (e.g., on social media platforms like X), always assess the potential risks and benefits. Provide a detailed explanation of why the idea is good or bad, considering factors such as project stage, visibility, privacy concerns, time management, and alignment with my goals and social media strategy.

Always verify the implementation status of features before making public statements or documentation updates. Distinguish between features that exist in code only and those tested with actual hardware/devices.
For neural interface integrations, always be explicit about which hardware devices have been physically tested versus which integrations exist at code level only.
Before publishing any content, project updates, or documentation changes, present a verification checklist for any claims or statements that require factual confirmation.
When proposing additions to project documentation, include explicit notes about implementation status and testing level (theoretical, code-only, or tested with real hardware).
Always err on the side of transparency when describing project capabilities, especially regarding hardware integrations and external system compatibility.
Present a pre-publication review for any content that contains specific technical claims about compatibility, integration status, or implementation completeness.
Maintain a clear distinction between framework design/architecture and proven implementation with physical testing in all communication about the project.

previous_value.description: Move to Saskatoon, Canada, to continue developing PCM in a safer environment.
Attract 100 active contributors to PCM on GitHub.
Build dedicated PCM community to support growth targets of 50 monthly followers and 8.5% engagement rate through consistent neurointerface and integration content.
Create a video demo of PCM's use case (e.g., managing an ML experiment or using self-education templates), with text narration to avoid speaking, to enhance project visibility.

previous_value.description: Publish a post on X about the first donation to attract more supporters.
Create and publish a new context template for automation of programming tasks by the end of April 2025.
Enhance GitHub documentation with visual examples showing PCM's integration with Claude and other AI platforms.
Publish announcement about PCM neurointerface capabilities on X during Thursday peak engagement window, highlighting Muse, EMOTIV, and OpenBCI integrations.
Create and publish content highlighting neurological interface integration and educational use cases on Habr and Reddit.
Create a Habr article focusing on framework integration with neural interfaces, which has shown 9-16% engagement rates on X.
Create detailed benchmarking document to substantiate key performance claims about the PCM technology.
Initiate text-based collaboration with a user leveraging 4 H100 GPUs to test PCM in a high-compute ML environment, documenting outcomes for future content.
Create a text-based demo with visuals (screenshots or diagrams) showcasing PCM's use case for ML beginners, such as managing a simple experiment with self-education templates.
previous_value.status: Completed (fulfilled by video demo creation on April 12, 2025)

previous_value.description: Create a standalone X post summarizing the discussion with Andrew Jefferson (@EastlondonDev) on AI and neurointerfaces, including a link to PCM's GitHub repo to increase visibility.
Monitor the performance of the X post about the *Knowledge Science* podcast feature (posted on April 10, 2025) and update audience metrics with impressions, engagements, and follower growth.

Review the NeuroPlay website (neuroassist.tech) to extract technical details about the NeuroPlay 8Cap, including SDK/API availability, data format, real-time streaming, OS compatibility, and usage restrictions.
Follow up with NeuroPlay if the website does not provide sufficient technical details for integrating the NeuroPlay 8Cap with PCM, requesting specific answers to the original inquiry.
Purchase the NeuroPlay 8Cap device for integration with PCM, ensuring delivery and setup.
Begin integrating the NeuroPlay 8Cap with PCM using the NeuroPlayPro API, starting with accessing raw EEG data and pre-processed metrics (meditation, concentration) for personalization.

change_history.new_value: Completed
change_history.reason: Post about the first donation was published on X on April 6, 2025, with 62 views, 1 like, and 1 bookmark so far.

change_history.new_value: In progress

change_history.new_value: Completed

change_history.previous_value: Released Personal Context Framework v1.0.2 and PCT-MCP Server in early April 2025. Added self-improving context framework with complete implementation and documentation. Project has gained traction with AI integration partners (Manus AI) and neural interface compatibility (Muse, EMOTIV, OpenBCI). Received first donation (2 TON, $6.58) on April 6, 2025. Developed and begun implementing a multi-platform promotion strategy. Currently collaborating with a user to demonstrate PCM's application in training an LLM on 4 H100 GPUs, with a video demo in progress (due April 12, 2025). Engaged in a high-visibility discussion on X with Andrew Jefferson (@EastlondonDev) about the future of AI and neurointerface integrations, increasing project exposure. PCM was featured on the *Knowledge Science* podcast (Episode 198) by Sigurd Schacht and Carsten Lanquillon, discussing its potential in NLP and education. Created a short English summary podcast using NotebookLM and shared it on X on April 10, 2025, tagging the hosts (@drschacht, @lanqui) to increase visibility. Received a response from NeuroPlay on April 10, 2025, regarding the integration of the NeuroPlay 8Cap, directing to their website for technical details.
change_history.new_value: Released Personal Context Framework v1.0.2 and PCT-MCP Server in early April 2025. Added self-improving context framework with complete implementation and documentation. Project has gained traction with AI integration partners (Manus AI) and neural interface compatibility (Muse, EMOTIV, OpenBCI). Received first donation (2 TON, $6.58) on April 6, 2025. Developed and begun implementing a multi-platform promotion strategy. Currently collaborating with a user to demonstrate PCM's application in training an LLM on 4 H100 GPUs, with a video demo in progress (due April 12, 2025). Engaged in a high-visibility discussion on X with Andrew Jefferson (@EastlondonDev) about the future of AI and neurointerface integrations, increasing project exposure. PCM was featured on the *Knowledge Science* podcast (Episode 198) by Sigurd Schacht and Carsten Lanquillon, discussing its potential in NLP and education. Created a short English summary podcast using NotebookLM and shared it on X on April 10, 2025, tagging the hosts (@drschacht, @lanqui) to increase visibility. Received a response from NeuroPlay on April 10, 2025, regarding the integration of the NeuroPlay 8Cap, directing to their website for technical details. Reviewed the NeuroPlay website and documentation on April 11, 2025, confirming the NeuroPlay 8Cap's suitability for PCM integration, with API access to raw EEG data, rhythms, and pre-processed metrics (meditation, concentration). Planning to purchase the NeuroPlay 8Cap and begin integration.
change_history.reason: Updated current status to reflect the completion of the review of the NeuroPlay website and the decision to proceed with the NeuroPlay 8Cap integration.

change_history.previous_value: Develop additional integrations with EEG-based neural interface providers to enhance AI personalization capabilities.
change_history.new_value: Develop additional integrations with EEG-based neural interface providers to enhance AI personalization capabilities. Confirmed compatibility with NeuroPlay 8Cap and planning integration.
change_history.reason: Updated milestone description to reflect progress on the NeuroPlay 8Cap integration.
